gap_attribution.method: Ch 8.2 telescoping split under realized prices: G = [J(d*|P) - J(d_rec|P)] + [J(d_rec|P) - J(d_act|P)]

forecast_reliability.status: Experimental – Not part of EDA Standard v1.0; report-only, not used to weight Audit Confidence.

opportunities.name: Dynamic dispatch trigger
opportunities.description: Respond to high-price windows instead of holding charge - replace static thresholds with a price-responsive trigger.
opportunities.evidence: 44 ledger row(s) classified 'Missed Arbitrage' with positive gap; sum of gap_step = 11,075.88 USD for the audited period.
opportunities.derivation: period_gain = sum of gap_step over ledger rows where decision_type maps to 'Missed Arbitrage' and gap_step > 0 (recorded period only; no forward projection).

opportunities.name: Raise dispatch to the available optimum
opportunities.description: During priced windows the asset dispatched below the feasible optimum - review threshold conservatism and power-limit settings.
opportunities.evidence: 4 ledger row(s) classified 'Partial Capture' with positive gap; sum of gap_step = 368.84 USD for the audited period.
opportunities.derivation: period_gain = sum of gap_step over ledger rows where decision_type maps to 'Partial Capture' and gap_step > 0 (recorded period only; no forward projection).

opportunities.name: Replace fixed-schedule logic
opportunities.description: Dispatch followed a predefined schedule rather than market signals during these intervals.
opportunities.evidence: 2 ledger row(s) classified 'Schedule-based Dispatch' with positive gap; sum of gap_step = 203.33 USD for the audited period.
opportunities.derivation: period_gain = sum of gap_step over ledger rows where decision_type maps to 'Schedule-based Dispatch' and gap_step > 0 (recorded period only; no forward projection).

opportunities.name: Reserve / frequency-market stacking
opportunities.description: Idle capacity may qualify for FCR / spinning-reserve products in markets that offer them.
opportunities.experimental_note: Experimental - Not part of EDA Standard v1.0. Not quantifiable from the audited dataset; no value figure is given.

opportunities.name: Curtailment-absorption charging
opportunities.description: Where colocated generation is curtailed, charging against curtailed energy may add value.
opportunities.experimental_note: Experimental - Not part of EDA Standard v1.0. Not quantifiable from the audited dataset; no value figure is given.

ai_commentary:
EXECUTIVE ASSESSMENT
During the audit period, Energy Asset captured 78.2% of its Theoretical Economic Ceiling (perfect-foresight upper-bound benchmark). Decision-quality risk band per the published DQ thresholds: Moderate.
The asset's dispatch decisions tracked the optimal counterfactual strategy closely throughout the audit period, with only minor deviations from peak economic performance.

KEY FINDINGS
✔ Decision Quality (ECF)             78.2 / 100
✔ Ceiling Gap (upper bound)          3,848.68 USD
✔ Recoverable Execution Gap          3,355.66 USD
✔ Missed High-Value Intervals         44
✔ Largest Evidence-Backed Action      Dynamic dispatch trigger

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS
The largest gap category is 'Missed Arbitrage' — 95.1% of the positive step-gap (exact partition of ledger rows by decision classification).
The ledger records 44 high-price windows classified 'Missed Arbitrage' — largest at 19:05, 20:05, 20:40 — where dispatch remained below the feasible optimum. Whether hardware, availability or contractual constraints contributed is OUTSIDE the scope of this data-only audit; the figures quantify the economic difference, not its operational cause.

OPERATIONAL IMPACT
The Recoverable Execution Gap for the audited period is 3,355.66 USD — value that was achievable using the day-ahead forecast available at decision time, with no additional hardware. The remaining 493.02 USD of the ceiling gap was reachable only with perfect price foresight and is NOT operator-attributable.

AUDITOR CONCLUSION
Based solely on the audited dataset, the asset captured 78.2% of its perfect-foresight ceiling (risk band: Moderate per the published DQ thresholds). This audit evaluates dispatch economics only; asset health, availability and contractual constraints are outside its evidence base.

RECOMMENDED ACTIONS (evidence-derived)

Recommendation 1 — Dynamic dispatch trigger
  Value at Stake          11,075.88 USD  (recorded period)
  Ledger Intervals        44
  Derivation              period_gain = sum of gap_step over ledger rows where decision_type maps to 'Missed Arbitrage' and gap_step > 0 (recorded period only; no forward projection).

Recommendation 2 — Raise dispatch to the available optimum
  Value at Stake          368.84 USD  (recorded period)
  Ledger Intervals        4
  Derivation              period_gain = sum of gap_step over ledger rows where decision_type maps to 'Partial Capture' and gap_step > 0 (recorded period only; no forward projection).

Recommendation 3 — Replace fixed-schedule logic
  Value at Stake          203.33 USD  (recorded period)
  Ledger Intervals        2
  Derivation              period_gain = sum of gap_step over ledger rows where decision_type maps to 'Schedule-based Dispatch' and gap_step > 0 (recorded period only; no forward projection).

ADVISORY DIRECTIONS — Experimental, not part of EDA Standard v1.0; not quantifiable from the audited dataset:
  • Reserve / frequency-market stacking: Idle capacity may qualify for FCR / spinning-reserve products in markets that offer them.
  • Curtailment-absorption charging: Where colocated generation is curtailed, charging against curtailed energy may add value.

GAP ATTRIBUTION (Ref. Manual Ch 8.2)
Theoretical Ceiling Gap    3,848.68 USD  — vs the perfect-foresight upper-bound benchmark.
  Forecast-Unreachable     493.02 USD  — capturable only with perfect price foresight (not operator-attributable).
  Recoverable Execution    3,355.66 USD  — achievable with information available at decision time (operator / automation attributable).
counterfactual_summary: Against the Theoretical Economic Ceiling (perfect-foresight upper-bound benchmark), Energy Asset could have captured 17,642.41 USD vs actual 13,793.73 USD — a ceiling gap of 3,848.68 USD. Of this, the Recoverable Execution Gap — value achievable using information available at decision time — is 3,355.66 USD; the remaining 493.02 USD was reachable only with perfect price foresight.

4 step(s) have negative prices — legitimate in some markets; the audit treats them as real.

Intent: arbitrage (1.00) ← default
